title: Cornerstone Event Record
description: Schema for .cornerstone/events/<name>.yaml — a single event registered in the project's local registry. Wraps an event signature with stable UUID, lifecycle status, and code locations where this event is emitted.

name.description: Event sub-type identifier; matches the file name (e.g. auth.login → events/auth.login.yaml). Stable; renaming is a breaking change for consumers.

uuid.description: Canonical event ID. Stable across renames; the value SIEM rules / dashboards / runbooks key off.

description.description: One-line description of what this event represents.

status.description: Lifecycle state. `deprecated` events still emit but should not have new emit_sites added. `removed` events are validated only for historical search; producing them in code is a lint error.

deprecation_note.description: Required when status is 'deprecated' or 'removed'. Explains why and what to migrate to.

signature.description: The event signature itself — references the canonical event-signature.json schema. Required fields: event.signature, configuration. Additional Cornerstone signature fields are passed through.

required_contexts.description: Context names that events using this signature must include.

emit_sites.description: Code locations where this event is emitted. Maintained automatically by the instrument and retrofit skills; humans should not hand-edit this list.

function.description: Enclosing function or method name; helps when a file is reformatted and line numbers shift.

last_seen.description: ISO 8601 date when the skill last verified this site is still emitting this event. Stale entries flagged by `corner-lint registry`.

examples.description: Sample event payloads for this signature. Used by `corner-lint validate` as fixtures and by the docs site for examples.
